name: Pricing Freemium Ladder
description: A staircase visualization where each tier sits one rung higher than the previous, connected by a dashed climb line. Reads as a journey instead of three brochure cards — useful for products with a true freemium funnel where the buyer needs to see the climb.

descriptions.descriptive: Cards positioned absolutely on a fixed-height stage so each step rises 80px above the previous one and shifts ~17% to the right. A dashed diagonal SVG line traces the climb. The recommended step inverts to a dark surface and lifts into a higher z-index, with a small primary 'Start here' pill that anchors the eye.
descriptions.usage: Use on freemium SaaS, creator tools, or developer products where the value of the funnel is the climb itself. Lands well between problem framing and FAQ; pairs poorly with the brochure-card pricing variants — pick one mental model per page.
descriptions.audienceFit: Self-serve buyers, indie hackers, and developer-tooling evaluators who appreciate seeing the full progression at once. Suits playful and bold brands; less suited to procurement-led B2B where a flat comparison table reads faster.
